assertions.expectation: Defines 'available' as versions officially available in the channel that customers can manually upgrade to
assertions.reason: Response clearly defines 'available' as 'Officially released in your release channel' and 'You CAN manually upgrade to this version'

assertions.expectation: Defines 'default' as the version used for new cluster creation — NOT necessarily the auto-upgrade target
assertions.reason: Response explicitly defines 'default' as 'Used for NEW cluster creation' and states 'Many users think "default" = "what my cluster upgrades to" — but that's wrong. Default is only for new cluster creation.'

assertions.expectation: Defines 'auto-upgrade target' as the version GKE will actually upgrade existing clusters to automatically
assertions.reason: Response defines 'auto-upgrade target' as 'What GKE will automatically upgrade YOUR cluster to' and 'What actually matters for upgrade planning'

assertions.expectation: Explains that default and auto-upgrade target are often the same but CAN differ, especially during new minor version rollouts
assertions.reason: Response explains they can differ and specifically states 'The auto-upgrade target can actually be LOWER than the default version during new minor version rollouts, as GKE phases in upgrades conservatively.'

assertions.expectation: Notes that the auto-upgrade target depends on cluster constraints (maintenance windows, maintenance exclusions)
assertions.reason: Response explicitly mentions 'taking into account: Your cluster's current constraints (maintenance windows, exclusions)'

assertions.expectation: Explains that a cluster with a 'no minor' exclusion will have a patch-only auto-upgrade target for its current minor
assertions.reason: Response mentions maintenance exclusions but does not specifically explain the 'no minor' exclusion scenario or patch-only upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking the cluster's auto-upgrade status to see the actual target version
assertions.reason: Response provides a specific gcloud command to check the cluster's auto-upgrade timeline and status

assertions.expectation: Points to the GKE release schedule or release notes for tracking version availability
assertions.reason: Response does not mention or reference the GKE release schedule or release notes as resources for tracking version availability
